phrasalVerbs.definition: To make a copy of information on your computer.
examples: Make sure to back up your files regularly.
I back up all my photos to the cloud.
Have you backed up your work before updating the software?

phrasalVerbs.definition: To put information into a computer or other electronic machine using keys or a keyboard.
examples: Key in your password to access the system.
She keyed in the customer's details carefully.
Please key in the code shown on the screen.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To make a piece of equipment ready for use.
examples: It took me an hour to set up the new printer.
Can you help me set up my email account?
The technician will set up the network tomorrow.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To start or stop a machine, light, etc. working.
examples: Switch on the computer and wait for it to load.
Don't forget to switch off the lights before leaving.
She switched on the TV to watch the news.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To try using something such as a machine or product to find out whether it works correctly or is satisfactory.
examples: We need to test out the new software before launching it.
I'm testing out a new app for learning languages.
Let's test out the camera before the event.

fillInExercises.sentence: Remember to ___ ___ your documents before closing the program.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Back up' means to make a copy of data for safety.

fillInExercises.sentence: Please ___ in your username and password.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Key in' means to type information using a keyboard.

fillInExercises.sentence: The IT department will ___ ___ the new computers tomorrow.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Set up' means to make equipment ready for use.

fillInExercises.sentence: ___ on the monitor and check if it's working.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Switch on' means to turn on a device.

fillInExercises.sentence: Let's ___ ___ the new printer before we buy more.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Test out' means to try something to see if it works correctly.

fillInExercises.sentence: Don't forget to ___ off the computer when you leave.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Switch off' means to turn off a machine or device.

fillInExercises.sentence: I always ___ ___ my photos to an external hard drive.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Back up' means to create a copy of your data.

fillInExercises.sentence: I spent the morning ___ up my new laptop.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Set up' means to prepare equipment for use.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: I just got a new laptop. Can you help me [set up|set up] the software?

lines.speaker: Priya
lines.text: Sure! First, [switch on/off|switch on/off] the computer and wait for it to start.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Done. Now what? I need to [key in|key in] something.

lines.speaker: Priya
lines.text: Yes, [key in|key in] your email to create an account.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Great. Should I also [back up|back up] my old files to this computer?

lines.speaker: Priya
lines.text: Definitely! Let's [test out|test out] the transfer speed first.

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: My computer won't start. I've tried to [switch on/off|switch on/off] it but nothing happens.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Have you tried [switching on/off|switch on/off] it and on again?

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: Yes, multiple times. I hope I didn't lose my work. I forgot to [back up|back up] my files.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Let me [test out|test out] a different power cable.

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: Thanks. Do I need to [key in|key in] my password again after this?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Yes, once we [set up|set up] the connection, you'll need to log in again.
